title: Low-Cost Observability Baseline
description: Adopt metrics-first + sampled tracing + structured logs with retention/tiering to control observability spend.

cost.costNotes: Estimates are intentionally conservative; verify against current pricing before committing.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Low-cost observability focuses on controlling telemetry costs through sampling and retention policies. Min ($50) represents a small application (1-3 services) using provider free tiers plus minimal paid metrics/logs (e.g., AWS CloudWatch ~$20-30/month for metrics, Datadog free tier + $20-30 for basic logs). Max ($2000) represents a medium-scale production system (10-50 services) with sampled tracing, structured logs with 30-day retention, and metrics aggregation across multiple environments. This is intentionally lower than full observability ($5000+) due to sampling (90-95% trace reduction), log filtering/tiering (storing only errors/warnings), and retention policies (7-30 days vs 90+ days). Assumes OpenTelemetry for vendor flexibility and cost optimization through filtering before egress.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity adoption requiring: (1) OpenTelemetry SDK integration across services (instrumentation libraries, context propagation) - 40-60 hours; (2) Implementing sampling strategies (head-based, tail-based) and configuring collectors - 20-30 hours; (3) Establishing log structuring standards (JSON formatting, field normalization) - 15-25 hours; (4) Setting up retention/tiering policies and cost alerting - 10-15 hours; (5) Training team on metrics-first debugging and trace analysis - 8-12 hours. Total 93-142 hours at $25-35/hour blended rate (with AI coding assistants reducing manual configuration by 30-40%). Lower than full observability ($5000+) because pattern explicitly limits scope to cost-effective essentials.
provenance.licenseCost: Open-source based pattern using OpenTelemetry (Apache 2.0 license), Prometheus (Apache 2.0), Grafana (AGPL/free tier), or similar CNCF tools. Pattern is cloud/vendor-agnostic and designed to work with native cloud provider tools (CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Cloud Logging) or open-source alternatives. Commercial observability platforms (Datadog, New Relic, Honeycomb) may be used but pattern emphasizes free tiers and usage-based pricing rather than upfront licensing. No mandatory commercial licenses required.

provides.reasoning: This pattern specifically focuses on implementing observability while controlling costs through metrics-first approach, sampling, and retention policies. This capability accurately describes the pattern's primary value proposition.
The pattern explicitly mentions 'sampled tracing' as a core technique for reducing observability costs. Providing a sampling strategy is a key implementation detail of this pattern.
The pattern explicitly mentions 'retention/tiering' as a cost control mechanism, providing policies and strategies for managing log data lifecycle.
The pattern includes retention/tiering as a core technique for cost control, implementing different storage tiers based on data age and access patterns.
This pattern establishes a foundational observability approach combining metrics, traces, and logs that other patterns could build upon while maintaining cost effectiveness.

requires.reasoning: The pattern adopts a 'metrics-first' approach, which requires an underlying capability to collect and store metrics. This is foundational to the pattern's implementation.
The pattern explicitly mentions 'structured logs' as part of the observability baseline. A structured logging capability must exist to implement this pattern effectively.
The pattern mentions 'sampled tracing', which requires an underlying distributed tracing capability. The pattern adds sampling on top of base tracing functionality.

The metrics-first approach requires a backend that stores and queries time-series metric data. Prometheus (part of obs-telemetry-backend) provides this storage natively — this is distinct from application-level time-series databases (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB) which serve different use cases.

supports_nfr.reason: Sampling and metrics-first observability can achieve up to four nines availability detection.
Structured logs with metrics enable root cause analysis within 10+ minute RTO windows.
Metrics scrape intervals and log buffer flush (1-5 min) align with RPO >= 5 minutes.
Sampling and tiering strategies handle up to ~28 jobs/second (100K/hour) without excessive cost.
Metrics-first observability is compatible with GDPR compliance requirements when properly configured.
Low-cost observability can be configured to meet HIPAA requirements with appropriate retention policies.
Observability baseline supports CCPA compliance through proper data retention and access controls.
Cost-optimized observability can support SOX compliance with appropriate retention and audit trails.

supports_constraints.reason: Observability baseline applies to any cloud environment or on-premises deployment.
Observability patterns apply to applications written in any language with metrics, logs, and traces.
Observability strategies apply to all platform types from web apps to batch jobs.

metrics_retention_days.description: How long to retain metrics data. Longer retention increases storage costs but enables historical analysis and trend detection

trace_sampling_rate.description: Percentage of traces to capture (0.01 = 1%). Lower rates reduce costs dramatically but may miss rare issues. Use 1.0 only for low-traffic services

log_retention_days.description: How long to retain logs in hot storage. Shorter retention significantly reduces costs. Consider tiering to cold storage for compliance needs

log_sampling_strategy.description: Which logs to keep. 'error-always-info-sampled' keeps all errors but samples info logs. 'adaptive' increases sampling during incidents

metrics_aggregation_interval.description: Seconds between metric data points. Higher intervals reduce cardinality and costs but decrease resolution for troubleshooting

enable_log_tiering.description: Move older logs to cheaper cold storage automatically. Reduces costs 80-90% but increases query latency for historical data

high_value_trace_retention_days.description: Retention for error traces and high-latency requests. Keep longer than sampled traces to debug recurring issues

sampled_trace_retention_days.description: Retention for successfully sampled traces. Short retention minimizes costs while maintaining recent visibility
